articles.author: Lorian Belanger, Brian P. D. Hannon
articles.description: Explosions sounded over Dubai early Tuesday as the United Arab Emirates’ military worked to intercept incoming Iranian fire that caused the country to briefly close its airspace as Israel launched new strikes in war in the Middle East.
articles.title: Live updates: UAE briefly closes airspace over Iranian attacks as Israel strikes Beirut and Tehran

articles.author: Anton L. Delgado, Aniruddha Ghosal
articles.description: The escalating Iran war is pushing parts of the world into energy triage. Governments are deciding where to cut demand or absorb higher costs, while prioritizing dwindling supplies. In places like Bangkok and Vietnam, people are being urged to work from home, take the stairs and keep air-conditioning above 24°C, while some businesses are preparing for disruptions. The conflict is disrupting critical oil and natural gas shipments from the Middle East, sending prices soaring and threatening global growth — with Asia facing the greatest risk from the shock. That prompted some governments to draw on their emergency reserves, a stopgap measure that can only go on for so long.
articles.title: Countries face energy triage as the Iran war escalates

articles.author: Collin Binkley
articles.description: President Donald Trump is delaying a diplomatic trip to China that was planned for months but began to unravel as he pressured Beijing and other world powers to form a military coalition to protect the vital Strait of Hormuz. Trump said Tuesday while meeting with Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin in the Oval Office that he would be going to China in five or six weeks’ time instead of at the end of the month. Trump’s visit to China is seen as an opportunity to build on a fragile trade truce between the two superpowers, but it became tangled in his effort to find an endgame to his war in Iran.
articles.title: Trump delays China trip to focus on war in Iran

articles.author: Jon Gambrell, David Rising, Samy Magdy
articles.description: Israel says it has killed two senior Iranian security officials in overnight strikes in a major blow to the country’s leadership. Tehran fired new salvos of missiles and drones at its Gulf Arab neighbors and Israel. Ali Larijani was the secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council. Gen. Gholam Reza Soleimani was the head of the Revolutionary Guard’s all-volunteer Basij force. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz confirmed their deaths but Iranian state media did not. The killings would strip Iran of important leaders as the Islamic Republic faces its greatest test in recent decades.
articles.title: Israel says it has killed two top Iranian security officials

articles.author: Jon Gambrell, Mae Anderson
articles.description: The Strait of Hormuz is a small strip of water connecting the Persian Gulf to the world’s oceans, and it has become a big problem for the global economy. On a typical day, ships carrying about a fifth of the world’s oil sail out of the Gulf through the narrow passageway. But the war with Iran means it’s effectively closed. The snarls have sent oil prices above $100 per barrel and threatened a surge of painful inflation for the global economy if the blockages last a long time. Some energy analysts believe oil prices could quickly jump to $150 per barrel if the strait remains closed
articles.title: Crude oil prices swing amid tensions in Strait of Hormuz

articles.author: Seung Min Kim, David Klepper
articles.description: President Donald Trump is pushing back against claims by the director of the National Counterterrorism Center about the motivations for the Iran war. In announcing his resignation Tuesday, Joe Kent's claimed Iran “posed no imminent threat" to the U.S. Trump says Iran is a “tremendous threat." Kent also said it was clear the U.S. started the war “due to pressure from Israel.” The Republican president previously has denied Israel forced the U.S. to act. Kent is a former Washington state political candidate with connections to right-wing extremists. As head of the National Counterterrorism Center, Kent was in charge of an agency tasked with analyzing and detecting terrorist threats.
articles.title: Top counterterrorism official Joe Kent resigns over Trump's Iran war

articles.author: Jon Gambrell
articles.description: Satellite photos are beginning to be released showing ships ablaze in an Iranian port and destroyed buildings at military bases. The images come from Planet Labs PBC, a San Francisco-based firm used widely by media outlets, including The Associated Press. Planet Labs has said it will put a two-week delay on its imagery becoming public citing the “increasingly complex regional security environment” and concerns its imagery could be used by “adversarial actors.” Information about the damage done across the wider Middle East in the Iran war remains scarce.
articles.title: Satellite photos show damage in Iran war

articles.author: Milexsy Durán
articles.description: Much of Cuba is without power after its third islandwide blackout in four months. The outage on Tuesday underscored its deepening energy and economic crises and political tensions. U.S. President Donald, who has called Cuba a “very weakened nation,” said on Monday he believes he’ll have the “honor of taking Cuba.” The island's government blames its woes on a U.S. energy blockade after Trump in January warned of tariffs on any country that sells or provides oil to Cuba. The country's aging grid has drastically eroded in recent years, leading to daily outages.
articles.title: Latest Cuban blackout leaves 11 million without power

articles.author: Ramon Espinosa
articles.description: HAVANA, Cuba (AP) — Officials in Cuba reported an islandwide blackout Monday as deepening energy and economic crises continue to strain a crumbling power grid.
articles.title: Cuba plunged into islandwide blackout as power crisis worsens, in photos

articles.description: President Donald Trump said Monday he believes he will soon have “the honor of taking Cuba,” but declined to say what that would involve.
articles.title: Watch: 'I do believe I'll have the honor of taking Cuba,' Trump says
